title: Kafka Log Cleaner Backlog and Compaction Errors
app: Splunk Add-on for Kafka (`Splunk_TA_kafka`, Splunkbase 2935) file monitoring
spl:
index=kafka sourcetype="kafka:logCleanerLog"
| rex "^\[[^\]]+\]\s+(?<log_level>\w+)"
| where log_level IN ("WARN","ERROR")
| stats count by host, log_level
| where count > 0
description: Cleaner WARN/ERROR lines signal compaction stalls, I/O errors deleting segments, or topics that cannot keep up with delete-retention—often precursors to disk exhaustion on compacted topics.
value: Prevents silent growth of uncompacted segments and highlights disks or file handles that are nearing limits on brokers running log compaction.
implementation: Enable collection of `log-cleaner.log` alongside other Kafka logs. For deeper coverage, join with host disk metrics. Tune thresholds per cluster; some WARN noise is normal during rebalance.
visualization: Timeline (cleaner errors), Bar chart (count by host), Table (recent raw messages).

references.title: Source types for the Splunk Add-on for Kafka

grandmaExplanation: We watch the housekeeping process that shrinks old message files so disks do not fill up quietly on busy topics.
